description_long: This object consists of a dense cluster of modern traffic light assemblies, each mounted on tall, slender metal poles. The poles are topped with curved arms supporting street lamps, and each pole features multiple traffic signal heads arranged at various heights and angles. The signal heads are rectangular and black, with visible lens sections for red, yellow, and green lights, and some poles also include smaller auxiliary signals. The arrangement suggests a complex intersection, with signals oriented to control multiple lanes and directions of traffic. The overall structure is metallic, with a matte gray finish on the poles and lamp arms, and a darker, matte black finish on the signal heads. The base of each pole is anchored to the ground, and the entire assembly is designed for durability and visibility in urban environments.
description: A cluster of tall, metal pole-mounted traffic lights with multiple black signal heads and integrated street lamps, arranged for a complex intersection.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the traffic lights from a diagonal angle, highlighting the staggered arrangement of poles and the multiple signal heads facing different directions.
description_view_1: View 1 presents a rear perspective, emphasizing the alignment of the poles and the back sides of the signal heads, with the street lamps clearly visible on top.
description_view_2: View 2 offers a side angle, showcasing the curved lamp arms and the layered positioning of the signal heads along the poles.
description_view_3: View 3 provides a top-down, slightly angled view, revealing the density of the signal heads and the spatial distribution of the poles.
